# Deploying Spire Proposals to Cloudflare Workers

This app is **TanStack Start** running SSR on **Cloudflare Workers** with
**Workers Static Assets** for the client bundle. Everything is generated by the
Vite/Nitro build — there is no separate SPA output and no Cloudflare Pages step.

Do NOT try to convert this to a static SPA: `/sitemap.xml` and per-route
`<head>` metadata (title, description, OG tags) are rendered by the Worker at
request time. Static hosting would break both.

---

## 1. One-time setup

Install Wrangler once, globally or via `bunx`:

```bash
bun add -g wrangler        # or: npm i -g wrangler
wrangler login
```

## 2. Build

```bash
bun install
bun run build
```

This produces:

```
dist/
├── client/          → static assets (served by Workers Static Assets binding "ASSETS")
│   ├── assets/
│   ├── _headers
│   └── robots.txt
└── server/          → the Worker
    ├── index.mjs    → Worker entry (main)
    ├── wrangler.json → generated deploy config (do NOT hand-edit)
    └── _chunks/, _ssr/, _libs/ …
```

`dist/server/wrangler.json` is regenerated on every build. It already wires:

- `main: index.mjs`
- `assets: { binding: "ASSETS", directory: "../client" }`
- `compatibility_flags: ["nodejs_compat"]`
- a fresh `compatibility_date`

## 3. Deploy

```bash
bun run cf:deploy              # = wrangler deploy --cwd dist/server
# or, build + deploy in one shot:
bun run cf:build-and-deploy
```

First deploy prompts to create the Worker. Subsequent deploys are instant.

## 4. Local preview against the Workers runtime

`bun run dev` uses Vite's dev server. To test the actual Worker bundle:

```bash
bun run build
bun run cf:preview             # = wrangler dev --cwd dist/server
```

Put local secrets in `.dev.vars` (copy `.dev.vars.example`) — Wrangler loads
them automatically. `.dev.vars` is git-ignored.

## 5. Environment variables & secrets

Two categories — they are set in different places:

| Kind | Where read | How to set (prod) | How to set (local) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Server secrets** (e.g. `SITE_URL`, API keys) — read via `process.env.X` inside `createServerFn` `.handler()` or a server route handler | Worker runtime | `wrangler secret put SITE_URL --cwd dist/server` | add to `.dev.vars` |
| **Client config** (`VITE_*`) — inlined into the browser bundle at build time | Browser | Set in shell **before** `bun run build`, or in your CI env | Set in shell / `.env.local` |

Never prefix a secret with `VITE_` — it will be shipped to the browser.

Currently used:

- `SITE_URL` — canonical origin for `/sitemap.xml` (e.g. `https://spireproposals.com`).

## 6. Custom domain

In the Cloudflare dashboard → Workers & Pages → your Worker → **Settings →
Domains & Routes** → **Add Custom Domain**. Cloudflare provisions TLS
automatically. No code changes needed.

## 7. Routing

You do **not** need `_redirects`, `wrangler.toml`, or SPA fallback rules.
The Worker itself handles every request through TanStack Router, so deep links
(`/about`, `/services`, `/contact`, `/privacy`, `/terms`, `/sitemap.xml`) work
on refresh and direct navigation with no extra config.

## 8. Runtime constraints (already satisfied)

The Cloudflare Workers runtime (V8 isolate + `nodejs_compat`) has no
`child_process`, no native modules (`sharp`, `canvas`, `puppeteer`), and no
`fs.watch`. This project uses none of them — only React, TanStack, Radix UI,
Zod, `date-fns`, `lucide-react`, `recharts` — all pure-JS and Worker-safe.

---

## Common CLI reference

```bash
wrangler deploy         --cwd dist/server           # deploy
wrangler dev            --cwd dist/server           # local Worker
wrangler tail           --cwd dist/server           # live logs
wrangler secret put X   --cwd dist/server           # add/update secret
wrangler secret list    --cwd dist/server
wrangler versions list  --cwd dist/server
wrangler rollback       --cwd dist/server
```
